message:
fix: harden async primitives against stale wakers, premature stream termination, and panic-induced arena corruption

Watch channel (src/channel/watch.rs):
- Deduplicate waker registrations by Arc pointer identity to prevent
  unbounded growth of the waiters list when the same receiver re-polls
- Refresh stored waker on re-poll so that executor migrations (e.g.,
  work-stealing runtimes) always wake the current task, not a stale one
- Add CountWake-based test proving only the latest waker fires after send

Chain reader (src/io/read.rs):
- Return Ok(()) immediately when ReadBuf has zero remaining capacity,
  preventing Chain from incorrectly interpreting a zero-byte read as EOF
  and prematurely switching from the first reader to the second
- Replace take_limits_reads test with chain_does_not_switch_on_full_buffer
  test that directly validates the zero-capacity edge case

Reactor API (src/runtime/reactor/linux.rs):
- Align IoUringReactor and EpollReactor stubs with updated Reactor trait
  signature: register() now accepts a Token parameter and returns ()
  instead of Registration; deregister() now accepts Token instead of
  Registration — keeps Linux stubs in sync with the cross-platform trait

ReceiverStream (src/stream/receiver_stream.rs):
- Add terminated flag so that once the channel returns Disconnected or
  Cancelled the stream fuses and always yields None, even if the
  underlying channel is later reconnected or cancel is cleared
- Add test proving the stream stays terminated after cancel-clear + send

WatchStream (src/stream/watch_stream.rs):
- Mirror the same fused-termination semantics: once the watch channel
  errors, the stream permanently yields None
- Add test module with watch_stream_none_is_terminal_after_cancel

Arena (src/util/arena.rs):
- Defer self.len increment until after the value is successfully
  constructed in both insert() and insert_with(), so a panic inside the
  user-supplied closure does not leave len out of sync with actual
  occupied slots
- In insert_with() for the free-list path, extract next_free/generation
  before calling the closure and only mutate free_head and the slot
  after the closure returns, preventing a half-mutated arena on panic
- Add tests for panic-safety in both fresh-slot and reused-slot paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
